reviews.review_text: There's something extremely satisfying about this game that makes it impossible for me to stop playing once I start. What sounds like a simple cleaning game somehow turns into one of the most relaxing, rewarding, and genuinely addictive games I've owned. Every level scratches that perfect "just one more section" itch, and watching the grime disappear with each spray is weirdly therapeutic. The sound design, the satisfying and rewarding ding when a section is complete, and the tiny details hidden beneath the dirt make every map feel exciting instead of repetitive. I love how calming this game is while still feeling incredibly rewarding, and its honestly become one of my favorite games to throw on when I want to relax, zone out, and feel productive without stressing myself out. Somehow cleaning virtual objects with a power washer became peak gaming for me and I regret absolutely nothing.

i used to love this game so much, to bad the devs are really greedy, they lock achievements behind DLC's, they made a whole second game that's legit the exact same as the first one and immediately started pumping DLC's for it, like come on, it's not that hard to be a good game studio, they get so blinded by the money that they forget what even made them succeed in the first place, i would 100% NOT recommend this game or any other game FuturLab try's to sell.
sooo the soap being limited in stock kinda sucks so i just played the entire game with just water.

Powerwash Simulator is a very cozy and relaxing game. Perfect game to turn off your brain and just powerwash everything clean. It has a lot of different levels to clean. It plays perfectly on the steam deck!
This is the video game you play when you’re not in the mood to play video games. the ultimate decompression activity.

This game is probably one of my favorite games of all time. I find completing mindless tasks like the powerwashing that is the main gameplay loop super satisfying, and I got addicted to this game very fast.

I will say, my wrist is sensitive due to over use (artist moment), and thus I actually can't play too much of this game in one sitting or else my wrist starts hurting from the repetitive cleaning motions. I had to take a lot of breaks and slowly make my way through in chunks. But its a true testament to how addictive and satisfying the gameplay loop is that I made sure I got through every single level and then played every single DLC I could. I LOVE this game, and I am currently making my way through Powerwash Simulator 2 as well (which I think is also wonderful, but thats a review for another time). 

I recommend this game if you enjoy mindless gameplay loops, the satisfaction of a job well done, and some silly banter from the npcs to the side as you go!
Didn't expect this silly game to get me emotional in the end, especially after playing through entirety of career mode with friend.

even after 4 years this game does feel a bit rough around the edges
particularly with its movement, as your character can often get stuck in place when trying to move sideways, and often gets stuck walking up stairs
though it would have been a nice to have side movement on ladders as well

there could have been more to sell the illusion of using a power wash, like droplets falling in an arc, which would slowly clean something rather than just being out of range
as well as better splattering up from the point of contact, and more waterflow effects even if they don't do anything 

the ending was fine, roughly what i expected them to do, so the game overall is also fine, but at least there is fun to be had by seeing all that dirt and grime disappear

After 100% this game I can finally say I am free!
Really great game, some fun wonkiness when playing with friends (I have gone to space more times than the Americians at this point) and overall a great experiance. Graphics are of a high quality, but my partner with a terrible computer was able to play flawlessly.

I am finally free from this cleaning hell!
Oh the new one is out.... back to cleaning!

The game is too inaccurate! Power washing on the first mission, I could wash the same part back and forth and the mud didn't vanish. Then on the second mission parts became 100% clean before all mud was gone. Just terrible!
Solid fun game to chill to and watch YouTube alongside etc but why at the end credits watching my work go by did i want to almost cry i think it was the song.

[h1]Carpal Tunnel Galore[/h1]
The game is great if you want something to just turn off your brain and relax. You just put on a youtube video, watch a show/movie or just listen to some music and go ham. It's honestly a really chill time.

As of writing this review I've now 100% the game in terms of official/DLC missions and getting all the achievements and I've had a blast getting through it all. It's great to sit down with a friend in co-op mode and just talk and wash and it's also a nice relaxing experience to just play alone and collect those satisfying dings. All in all a pretty good game that I can strongly recommend.
A very satisfying and relaxing game. It's a good break from the regular, high intensity games I like to play. This game is great if you're looking for an easy, low stress game to just have fun with!
Surprisingly fun (IRL not so much); great casual, no rush game.  Mechanics are sound;  user friendly.  Task orientated, earn $$$, upgrade equipment, get er'done!
Great game to play with friends. This is phssssswwww satisfactory.

The second time the work around did not work and sadly the helicopter level is now broken.  Was playing the game with my son and now we can't move forward with the game.  It's fun other than this glitch.

I'd recommend this game alot but i do give you a warning: if your hoping NOT to sink hours into this game you will not be getting away with that but overall the games pretty good and personally relaxing to me

33 hours in and It's a great game so far. I usually put on some music on and turn my brain off for a bit. I did struggle in the beginning finding the last few things on the list to clean but ultimately I figured it out. I'd recommend to people looking for a solid game you can spend some time on without the sweats of online shooter games or other competitive stuff. Just you, your power washer and the dirt that needs to get taken care of!
what it says on the tin and then some! i love this game and intend on resetting so i can do career mode again for the first time if i can figure it out lol

This is one of my favorite games, I find it very soothing. I love the expansions that they have created for it and the level of detail it requires you to focus on obtaining. When I worked a very high-stress/high-stakes job, I frequently played this after work to self-soothe- it's even better now that I just play for fun!

Facing your inner demons? Having a depressive spiral? Want to stop thinking? Well, I have great news for you, buddy. Just play this game. Unironically when I get bad, I play this game. Nothing feels so bad after cleaning up something that isn't your room because that's too much energy you don't have at the time. But you still feel like you accomplished something even if it's silly. I love u powerwash game. 

Regretfully, your switch version not having motion controls makes trying to play it on switch really unpleasant though. That's the single complaint I have and it's not even relevant to Steam.

PowerWash Simulator stands out as genuinely one of the most satisfying games you can play, offering a remarkably soothing and therapeutic experience. Thanks to a steady stream of updates and DLC, there is a massive amount of content to get stuck into. The game strikes a very nice balance between location sizes and vehicles, ensuring that none of the jobs ever feel like they will drag on forever. Adding to the charm of the world are the occasional text messages you receive during jobs, which reveal that the local characters are wonderfully eccentric. The DLC crossovers are a massive highlight, and it is awesome to experience the sheer scale of iconic places like Seventh Heaven, Shrek's Swamp, and the Thunderhawk. Furthermore, the progression from washer to washer feels steady enough that you are never stuck with equipment that cannot handle new dirt types for very long.

Despite the highly enjoyable core gameplay, there are a few notable frustrations to be found across a long playthrough. The Gnome Fountain level is a bit of a slog because it is massive, yet you are assigned the Urban X U2 washer which is just not well suited for a job of that scale, making it a place where the Vista 3000 would have been much better. For some reason, most of the DLC packs restrict players from using the Triple Nozzle, which is an odd choice that makes the jobs needlessly tedious unless you use a mod to enable it. There are also only four power washers in total, meaning that if you intend to complete every job in the game, you will be stuck looking at the final Vista PRO model for a very long time. Finally, a couple of times over the course of a one hundred hour playthrough, I encountered the annoying invisible dirt glitch where the final tiny percentage of grime would not show up even when using the dirt highlighting button.

Overall PowerWash Simulator is an incredibly satisfying and addictive experience that succeeds on its simple premise and fantastic selection of collaborative crossover content. While the limited washer variety, occasional microscopic dirt bugs, and restrictive DLC nozzle choices can occasionally disrupt the relaxation, the sheer volume of content and therapeutic gameplay loop make it a must play title for anyone looking to unwind.

I got this game becuase is was on sale and streamed it to my soon to be wife. 
I gifted her this game so she can join me.

She's addicted to the game now and now I'm working less than minimum wage while she gets full profit.

This game is a good way to destress and take a break. I find the lore is stable and straight forward and the  gameplay is calming.

This is the ultimate stress-relief simulator, did you have a ♥♥♥♥ day and want to rip the skin from the base of your skull? Load this game up and you will forget what the concept of time even is let alone your day to day problems. I loaded into the fire station mission, zoned out while washing and 2 hours had suddenly gone by, did I care? Not really, this game is also great if you wanna binge a show and still feel like you're doing something.

Game is great, nothing more relaxing than washing dirt away and jamming out to tunes to wind down for the night. Would recommend to anyone looking for a relaxing time and needing a break.

For anybody who needs a game that is easy, simple, but still has some challenges (that aren't too hard), this is the perfect game for you. It's especially perfect for those with difficulties on focusing or patience, such as ADHD or Autism. 11/10 game, highly recommend.

If any game could be compared to having a a gold brick wrapped in a lemon thrown at your head, well this is the one. Many will refer this as being the best bang (for your buck) since the big one. 

Ok, that's enough plagiarizing of Douglas Adam's work. In all honesty, this game is just a satisfying waste of time. I could not recommend it more.
Super satisfying ,relaxing (except when it feels impossible to find the dirt at the end of some levels) and helps learning patience and focus. :D

Have you ever wanted to feel the satisfaction of doing manual labor, but are too lazy, unmotivated, or physically impaired to do so? Well, then this game is for you! I assure you, this game truly captures the magic of backbreaking blue-collar work. Though, it is a shame you can't feel the sensations of the job. Sunburn, Sore Limbs, and a Strained Neck really make the whole experience that much better (worse).
I thought this is a game where you wash things while furiously yelling "POWEEEEER". It is not. The neighbours have filed a noise complaint. If this review ends abruptly, i have been apprehe

I gave this game almost two days of my life ! Two days !

And I enjoyed it, the level were nice, cozy, interesting and sometimes challenging you know, a relaxing time... then came the level in the forest, the one with the tree house... WHAT HAPPENED !? and I thought, that's all right, I can't enjoy all the level, then came the temple, the slide, the ferris wheel, oh my god the ferris wheel ! , and the subway station... started to be quite a lot.

Started to feel like work, I wasn't relaxing and enjoying cleaning, I was working and looking to every bit of small dirt in strange places. 

Even got a bug in the washroom, where I had to clean a whole space again to find a invincible piece of dirt.
Soooooo it's done, thank you, but I'm done !
